basic_details.job_title: Shrine Board Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Offline for 105 Junior Assistant, Storekeeper and More Posts
basic_details.short_information: The table below summarises the core facts of this Shrine Board recruitment drive that every applicant should know before reading further. SMVDSB Recruitment 2026 covers 105 vacancies across 12 different posts, all based in Katra. Every post carries the same application fee of ₹200/- per post, payable only through a Demand Draft, and the recruitment closes on 07 July 2026.

application_fee.details: This section explains how much candidates must pay and the exact mode of payment accepted for SMVDSB Recruitment 2026. | The application fee is ₹200/- per post for every post listed in this notice, and it must be paid through a Demand Draft in original, drawn in favour of the FA/Chief Accounts Officer, SMVDSB, Katra. There is no online payment option mentioned in the notice; the Demand Draft must be enclosed along with the physical application form.

age_limit.details: This section covers who is eligible by age and what kind of appointment SMVDSB is offering for these 105 posts. | Candidates must have attained 18 years of age and must not be more than 40 years old to be eligible, except for ex-servicemen, for whom the maximum age limit is 45 years. The appointment will be on a "Term Employment" basis, initially for a period of one year, which may be extended further subject to satisfactory performance, and shall be governed by the service conditions prescribed by the Shrine Board.

post_wise_qualification.essential_qualification: Diploma in Coaching from NIS Patiala or an equivalent qualification from a recognized university/institution; OR participation in Olympic/Asian Games/World Championship or an equivalent international event; OR a medal winner in Commonwealth Games/National Games/National Championship; OR a Dronacharya Awardee. Coaching experience in the concerned discipline at the national/international level is desirable.

Master's degree in Computer Science/Computer Applications/Information Technology, or B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science or IT from a recognized university; knowledge of programming languages such as C++, JAVA, .NET and MS Office; familiarity with recent programming software; and at least 2 years of post-qualification experience in computer hardware and networking, including installation/maintenance of switches, routers, hubs, LAN/WAN, internet leased lines, OFC, Wi-Fi networks, server administration and cyber security.
Graduation with a Diploma in Sanitation/Public Health, with 2 years of relevant experience.

Graduate with a one-year Diploma in Computer Applications from a recognized institute, or Bachelor's in Computers/Information Technology, with a minimum of 50% marks, a minimum typing speed of 35 w.p.m., and 3 years of experience in a relevant field.
Graduation with a minimum of 3 years of post-qualification professional experience in catering sales and service at a 3-star facility hotel/restaurant registered with a government department.
Graduate in Science/Arts/Commerce with at least 2 years of experience in the stores department of a government or public sector undertaking.
Graduate with 2 years of experience, a good personality, and the ability to speak English.
Matric with a Diploma from an I.T.I. in the relevant trade and 5 years of experience.
Bachelor's in Hotel Management with a minimum of 3 years of professional cooking experience at a 3-star facility hotel/restaurant registered with a government department.
Matric with 3 years of relevant experience.
Matric with 3 years of relevant experience.
A 10th-pass ex-serviceman with a good record in the Army/Navy/Air Force and a minimum of 15 years of service, or an Agniveer with 4 years of continuous service.

qualification.details: SMVDSB has grouped its 12 posts into three pay levels. This section presents the vacancies and qualification requirements post-by-post within each pay band, so candidates can quickly locate the post that matches their qualification. | Two posts fall under Level-8, both requiring specialised technical or sporting qualifications. | The largest share of vacancies falls under Level-4, covering seven posts that together account for more than half of the total openings in this recruitment. | Among Level-4 posts, Sales & Service Executive carries the highest vacancy count at 11, while Junior Assistant, Storekeeper and Senior Receptionist each have 8 posts. | Three posts fall under the entry-level Level-1 pay scale, including the largest single vacancy block in the entire notice. | The Enforcement & Security Guard post alone offers 30 vacancies - the single largest opening in this entire recruitment - but it is restricted exclusively to ex-servicemen and Agniveers meeting the specified service criteria. | In-Hand Salary Calculator

followed by a Written Test and Personal Interview. The notice does not specify a written marks weightage or syllabus. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official website regularly for shortlisting updates and the dates of the Written Test/Personal Interview
since no separate postal communication will be sent to candidates regarding these stages.
how_to_apply: Unlike many recent government recruitments, SMVDSB Recruitment 2026 does not use an online application portal. This section walks through the exact offline steps candidates must follow.
Prepare a written application along with complete Bio-Data, in original.
Attach self-attested copies of all required qualification certificates, experience certificate, and date-of-birth proof, along with two self-attested passport-size photographs.
Enclose a Demand Draft (in original) of ₹200/- per post, drawn in favour of the FA/Chief Accounts Officer, SMVDSB, Katra.
Ex-servicemen applicants must additionally enclose their Discharge Book.
Submit the complete application either by depositing it in person at the Central Office, SMVDSB, Katra (J&K) – 182301, or by sending it by post to the same address.
Ensure the application reaches the Central Office on or before 07 July 2026 - applications received after this date will be outrightly rejected, regardless of the postal dispatch date.

content_sections.heading: About Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board
content_sections.text: This section explains who is conducting SMVDSB Recruitment 2026 and under what official reference this notice was issued. The Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board is described in its own Employment Notice as a statutory autonomous body . It has released this recruitment notice as , dated 15 June 2026 , inviting applications from eligible candidates for 105 posts to be based out of its Central Office at Katra, Jammu & Kashmir – 182301.

content_sections.heading: Shrine Board Recruitment 2026 - Overview
content_sections.text: The table below summarises the core facts of this Shrine Board recruitment drive that every applicant should know before reading further. SMVDSB Recruitment 2026 covers 105 vacancies across 12 different posts , all based in Katra. Every post carries the same application fee of ₹200/- per post , payable only through a Demand Draft, and the recruitment closes on 07 July 2026 .

rows.details: Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board (SMVDSB)

content_sections.heading: Shrine Board Post-wise Vacancies and Eligibility (Grouped by Pay Level)
content_sections.text: SMVDSB has grouped its 12 posts into three pay levels. This section presents the vacancies and qualification requirements post-by-post within each pay band, so candidates can quickly locate the post that matches their qualification. Two posts fall under Level-8, both requiring specialised technical or sporting qualifications. The largest share of vacancies falls under Level-4, covering seven posts that together account for more than half of the total openings in this recruitment. Among Level-4 posts, Sales & Service Executive carries the highest vacancy count at 11 , while Junior Assistant, Storekeeper and Senior Receptionist each have 8 posts. Three posts fall under the entry-level Level-1 pay scale, including the largest single vacancy block in the entire notice. The Enforcement & Security Guard post alone offers 30 vacancies - the single largest opening in this entire recruitment - but it is restricted exclusively to ex-servicemen and Agniveers meeting the specified service criteria. In-Hand Salary Calculator

Coaching experience in the concerned discipline at the national/international level is desirable.

Master's degree in Computer Science/Computer Applications/Information Technology, or B.Tech/B.E. in Computer Science or IT from a recognized university; knowledge of programming languages such as C++, JAVA, .NET and MS Office; familiarity with recent programming software; and at least 2 years of post-qualification experience in computer hardware and networking, including installation/maintenance of switches, routers, hubs, LAN/WAN, internet leased lines, OFC, Wi-Fi networks, server administration and cyber security.
Graduation with a Diploma in Sanitation/Public Health, with 2 years of relevant experience.

Graduate with a one-year Diploma in Computer Applications from a recognized institute, or Bachelor's in Computers/Information Technology, with a minimum of 50% marks, a minimum typing speed of 35 w.p.m., and 3 years of experience in a relevant field.
Graduation with a minimum of 3 years of post-qualification professional experience in catering sales and service at a 3-star facility hotel/restaurant registered with a government department.
Graduate in Science/Arts/Commerce with at least 2 years of experience in the stores department of a government or public sector undertaking.
Graduate with 2 years of experience, a good personality, and the ability to speak English.
Matric with a Diploma from an I.T.I. in the relevant trade and 5 years of experience.
Bachelor's in Hotel Management with a minimum of 3 years of professional cooking experience at a 3-star facility hotel/restaurant registered with a government department.

content_sections.heading: Age Limit and Nature of Appointment
content_sections.text: This section covers who is eligible by age and what kind of appointment SMVDSB is offering for these 105 posts. Candidates must have attained 18 years of age and must not be more than 40 years old to be eligible, except for ex-servicemen, for whom the maximum age limit is 45 years . The appointment will be on a "Term Employment" basis , initially for a period of one year , which may be extended further subject to satisfactory performance, and shall be governed by the service conditions prescribed by the Shrine Board.

content_sections.heading: Application Fee and Payment Mode
content_sections.text: This section explains how much candidates must pay and the exact mode of payment accepted for SMVDSB Recruitment 2026. The application fee is ₹200/- per post for every post listed in this notice, and it must be paid through a Demand Draft in original , drawn in favour of the FA/Chief Accounts Officer, SMVDSB, Katra . There is no online payment option mentioned in the notice; the Demand Draft must be enclosed along with the physical application form.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply: Shrine Board Recruitment 2026 Application Process (Offline)
content_sections.text: Unlike many recent government recruitments, SMVDSB Recruitment 2026 does not use an online application portal. This section walks through the exact offline steps candidates must follow. Prepare a written application along with complete Bio-Data, in original. Attach self-attested copies of all required qualification certificates, experience certificate, and date-of-birth proof, along with two self-attested passport-size photographs. Enclose a Demand Draft (in original) of ₹200/- per post, drawn in favour of the FA/Chief Accounts Officer, SMVDSB, Katra. Ex-servicemen applicants must additionally enclose their Discharge Book. Submit the complete application either by depositing it in person at the Central Office, SMVDSB, Katra (J&K) – 182301 , or by sending it by post to the same address. Ensure the application reaches the Central Office on or before 07 July 2026 - applications received after this date will be outrightly rejected, regardless of the postal dispatch date.

content_sections.heading: Documents to Enclose with the Application
content_sections.text: This section lists every document SMVDSB requires to be attached with the application form at the time of submission itself. Application with complete Bio-Data (in original) Qualification Certificates/Degree(s) Experience Certificate Proof regarding date of birth (self-attested photocopy) Two self-attested passport-size photographs Demand Draft (in original) Discharge Book (applicable only for ex-servicemen) The notice is explicit that incomplete application forms will be outrightly rejected , so candidates should cross-check this list before sending or depositing their application.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process for Shrine Board Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: This section explains how SMVDSB will shortlist and finally select candidates for these 105 posts. Selection involves shortlisting of candidates , followed by a Written Test and Personal Interview . The notice does not specify a written marks weightage or syllabus. Candidates are advised to keep checking the official website regularly for shortlisting updates and the dates of the Written Test/Personal Interview, since no separate postal communication will be sent to candidates regarding these stages.

content_sections.heading: Important Terms and Conditions
content_sections.text: This section lists the administrative conditions SMVDSB has attached to this recruitment, which can affect the final number of posts filled or even the recruitment itself. The number of posts can increase or decrease without prior notice at any point before the completion of the selection process. The Shrine Board reserves the at any stage, for any administrative reason. Candidates must check the official website periodically for shortlisting status and test/interview dates, as no separate postal intimation will be issued. Incomplete application forms , and applications received after 07 July 2026 , will be outrightly rejected.

content_sections.heading: Notification and Official Website
content_sections.text: This section points candidates to where they can verify this notice and track further recruitment updates. This recruitment was issued as , signed by Shri Satish Kumar Sharma (JKAS), Joint Chief Executive Officer, SMVDSB. The notice does not provide a separate downloadable notification PDF link; candidates should treat the Shrine Board's official website as the authoritative source for verification and updates. PDF Download Link Application Form

faq.question: Q1. What is Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: It is an Employment Notice (Advertisement No. 05/2026, dated 15 June 2026) issued by the Shri Mata Vaishno Devi Shrine Board for 105 vacancies across 12 posts — including Assistant Coach, Programmer, Chef, Senior Electrician and Enforcement & Security Guard — based in Katra, Jammu & Kashmir.

faq.question: Q2. How many total vacancies are there in Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 105 vacancies in total, with Enforcement & Security Guard carrying the highest count at 30 posts, followed by Sales & Service Executive with 11 posts.

faq.question: Q3. Is Shrine Board Recruitment 2026 an online or offline application process?
faq.answer: It is an offline process. There is no online application portal; candidates must send or personally deposit their application, along with a Demand Draft, at the Shrine Board's Central Office in Katra.

faq.question: Q4. What is the last date to apply for Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The completed application must reach the Central Office, SMVDSB, Katra, on or before 07 July 2026. Applications received after this date will be outrightly rejected.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The application fee is ₹200/- per post, payable only through a Demand Draft drawn in favour of the FA/Chief Accounts Officer, SMVDSB, Katra.

faq.question: Q6. What is the age limit for Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be at least 18 years old and not more than 40 years old. The maximum age limit is relaxed to 45 years for ex-servicemen.

faq.question: Q7. What is the nature of appointment for Shrine Board posts?
faq.answer: All posts are on a "Term Employment" basis, initially for one year, extendable based on satisfactory performance and governed by the Shrine Board's prescribed service conditions.

faq.question: Q8. What is the selection process for Shrine Board Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection involves shortlisting of candidates, followed by a Written Test and a Personal Interview. Dates are announced only on the official website, with no separate postal communication sent to candidates.

faq.question: Q9. Where should the Shrine Board application be sent?
faq.answer: The application must be sent to, or deposited in person at, the Chief Executive Officer, SMVDSB, Central Office, Katra (J&K) – 182301.

faq.question: Q10. What documents must be enclosed with the Shrine Board application form?
faq.answer: Candidates must enclose complete Bio-Data, self-attested qualification certificates, experience certificate, date-of-birth proof, two self-attested passport-size photographs, the original Demand Draft, and (for ex-servicemen) the Discharge Book. Incomplete forms will be outrightly rejected.
